---
title: "Introduction to Bayesian Statistics"
subtitle: "1: Bayesian Models"
author:
  - name: Sena Tanaka
    email: sena.tanaka0210@gmail.com
    #orcid: 0000-0000-0000-0000
    affiliations:
    - name: Doshisha University
date: now
date-format: long
execute:
    echo: false
    warning: false
    message: false
    cache: false
format:
  revealjs:
    #incremental: true
    standalone: true
    embed-resources: true
    self-contained-math: true
    width: 1600
    height: 900
    theme: [default, css/quartomonothemer.scss, css/custom.scss]
    slide-number: true
    fig-cap-location: top
    fig-format: svg
    pdf-separate-fragments: false
knitr: 
  opts_chunk: 
    dev: ragg_png
    fig-align: center
    dpi: 144
    message: false
---

# 538 Problem
## Example: Predicting Election Outcomes

- The 538 Problem:
  - Predicts the winner in each state/district
  - Uses aggregate data from polls
    - Sample size
    - Number of intended votes for each candidate
    
- Model:
  - For each district $i$,
  - $p_i$: Dem's actual vote share
  - $X_{ij}$: Intended votes for Dem in poll $j$
  - $n_{ij}$: Sample size of poll $j$
  - Generative process: $X_{ij} \overset{\text{indep.}}{\sim} \text{Binom}(n_{ij}, p_i) \quad \text{for } j = 1, \ldots, J$

--- 

- Goal of Bayesian Inference:
  - `Posterior distribution` of $p_i$ given $X_{i1},...,X_{iJ}$
  
# Bayesian Inference
## Bayes' Theoram

- `Bayes' theoram`:
  - Parameter $\theta$with prior $p(\theta)$
  - Data $X$ with likehood $p(X|\theta)$
  - Likehood = joint distribution of data
  - Posterior of $\theta$ given $X$:
  
  $$p(\theta \mid \mathbf{X}) = \frac{p(\theta) p(\mathbf{X} \mid \theta)}{\int p(\theta) p(\mathbf{X} \mid \theta) \, d\theta}$$ {#eq-poisson}
